name: Slime.Engine

unions.name: Listener
unions.comment:
A Listener constructed in a similar way to Systems with a different purpose:
accepting messages through TEA. It has the same options as System, except Time.
cases: msg -> world -> ( world, Cmd msg, List EntityID )

unions.name: System
unions.comment:
A System constructed in this way will be used by Engine to update the world
with control over side effects (deleting entities and sending commands).

Each of the types has a different function signature for different options:
 * Time: Accepts deltaTime as its first argument.
 * Commands: The return value includes messages. The extra value is included as part of a tuple.
 * Deletes: The return value includes EntityIDs which should be deleted. The extra value is included as part of a tuple.

There is also a Basic, which has no options.
cases: Float -> world -> ( world, Cmd msg, List EntityID )
world -> ( world, Cmd msg, List EntityID )

aliases.name: Engine
aliases.comment:
The Engine type is used as the first argument of applySystems and applyListeners.

With the engine, update functions can be massively simplified, in a true ECS fashion.
aliases.type: { deleteEntity : EntityDeletor world, systems : List (System world msg), listeners : List (Listener world msg) }

values.name: applyListeners
values.comment:
A good way to use this is to curry the Engine out and use it in the catch-all
of your update function:
    runWorld = applySystems engine
And then later...
    _ ->
        (updatedWorld, cmd) = runWorld msg
values.type: Engine world msg -> world -> msg -> ( world, Cmd msg )

values.name: applySystems
values.comment:
A good way to use this is to curry the Engine out:
    runWorld = applySystems engine
And then later...
    (updatedWorld, cmd) = runWorld deltaTime
values.type: Engine world msg -> world -> Float -> ( world, Cmd msg )

values.name: initEngine
values.comment: Initializes an Engine with a deletor, a list of systems, and a list of listeners.
values.type: EntityDeletor world -> List (System world msg) -> List (Listener world msg) -> Engine world msg

name: Slime
comment:
Experimental

This library provides an easy way to construct entity-component-system style
codeflow in Elm. It achieves this mostly by leveraging currying and getter/setters. For example:

    type alias World =
        EntitySet
            { transforms : ComponentSet Rect
            , balls : ComponentSet Ball
            }

    transforms : ComponentSpec Rect World
    transforms =
        { getter: .transforms,
        , setter: (\world -> \comps -> {world | transforms = comps})
        }


    balls : ComponentSpec Ball World
    balls =
        { getter: .balls,
        , setter: (\world -> \comps -> {world | balls = comps})
        }

    moveBalls : Float -> World -> World
    moveBalls delta =
        -- ...
          stepEntities2 balls transforms (\ent2 -> { ent2 | b = addVelocity ent2.a ent2.b delta })

stepEntities2 goes through all the balls (any entity with a ball component and transform component)
in this example and updates their location based on their velocity and the time elapsed.

Because moveBalls' type signature has no concept of the components involved, these systems can easily be
composed to operate in sequence to create an ECS Engine.

# Types
@docs EntityID, EntitySet, ComponentSpec, ComponentSet, EntityDeletor, Entity, Entity2, Entity3

# Updates and Maps
@docs map, stepEntities, stepEntities2

# Initialization
@docs initComponents, initIdSource

# Deletion
@docs deleteEntity, (&->)

# Retrieval
@docs entities, entities2, getComponent

# Updates
@docs setEntity, setEntity2

# Creation
@docs spawnEmpty, spawnEntity, spawnEntity2, spawnEntities, spawnEntities2

aliases.name: ComponentSet
aliases.comment: Include as a field in your world and initialize with `.initComponents`

aliases.name: ComponentSpec
aliases.comment: A simple {getter, setter} record which is used as a building block for complex functions on `world`
aliases.type: { getter : world -> ComponentSet a, setter : world -> ComponentSet a -> world }

aliases.name: Entity2
aliases.type: { id : EntityID, a : a, b : b }

aliases.name: Entity3
aliases.type: { id : EntityID, a : a, b : b, c : c }

aliases.name: EntitySet
aliases.comment: Your world should be an EntitySet. Include an `.idSource` initialized with `initIdSource`

values.name: deleteEntity
values.comment:
Use as the start of a deletion block:

    deletor = deleteEntity transformSpec
        &-> massSpec
        &-> anotherSpec

The resulting deletor takes an EntityID and a world and clears the world of that Entity.

values.name: entities
values.type: ComponentSpec a world -> world -> List (Entity a)

values.name: entities2
values.comment: Simple entity retrieval. An entity is only included if both components provided exist for that entity.
values.type: ComponentSpec a world -> ComponentSpec b world -> world -> List (Entity2 a b)

values.name: getComponent
values.comment: Simple retrieval from a component set.

values.name: initIdSource
values.comment: Use to create an ID source for an EntitySet.

values.name: map
values.comment: Creates a list of something for each existing component of a given type.
values.type: ComponentSpec a world -> (a -> b) -> world -> List b

values.name: setEntity
values.comment: Sets the world with an entity's component updated.
values.type: ComponentSpec a world -> world -> Entity a -> world

values.name: setEntity2
values.comment: Sets the world with an entity's component updated.
values.type: ComponentSpec a world -> ComponentSpec b world -> world -> Entity2 a b -> world

values.name: spawnEmpty
values.comment:
Spawns an empty Entity. Useful if you just need an Entity ID and want to
set the components manually.
values.type: EntitySet world -> ( EntitySet world, EntityID, EntityID )

values.name: spawnEntities
values.comment: A convenience method to spawn multiple entities, provided as a list.
values.type: ComponentSpec a (EntitySet world) -> EntitySet world -> List { a : a } -> ( EntitySet world, List EntityID, List EntityID )

values.name: spawnEntities2
values.comment: A convenience method to spawn multiple entities, provided as a list.
values.type: ComponentSpec a (EntitySet world) -> ComponentSpec b (EntitySet world) -> EntitySet world -> List { a : a, b : b } -> ( EntitySet world, List EntityID, List EntityID )

values.name: spawnEntity
values.comment: Spawns an Entity with one component.
values.type: ComponentSpec a (EntitySet world) -> EntitySet world -> { a : a } -> ( EntitySet world, EntityID, EntityID )

values.name: spawnEntity2
values.comment: Spawns an Entity with two components.
values.type: ComponentSpec a (EntitySet world) -> ComponentSpec b (EntitySet world) -> EntitySet world -> { a : a, b : b } -> ( EntitySet world, EntityID, EntityID )

values.name: stepEntities
values.comment: Step entities based only on one component.
values.type: ComponentSpec a world -> (a -> a) -> world -> world

values.name: stepEntities2
values.comment: Step entities based on two component.
values.type: ComponentSpec a world -> ComponentSpec b world -> (Entity2 a b -> Entity2 a b) -> world -> world

binops.type: EntityDeletor (EntitySet world) -> ComponentSpec b (EntitySet world) -> EntityDeletor (EntitySet world)
